description: This event measures when a user is awarded virtual currency in a game. Log this along with spend_virtual_currency to better understand your virtual economy.

parameters.name: virtual_currency_name
parameters.description: The name of the virtual currency.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The value of the virtual currency.

description: Log this event when a user joins a group such as a guild, team, or family. Use this event to analyze how popular certain groups or social features are.

parameters.name: group_id
parameters.description: The ID of the group.

description: Send this event to signify that a user has logged in to your website or app.

parameters.name: method
parameters.description: The method used to login.

description: Log this event to indicate when the user has performed a search. You can use this event to identify what users are searching for on your website or app. For example, you could send this event when a user views a search results page after performing a search.

parameters.name: search_term
parameters.description: The term that was searched for.

description: This event signifies that a user has selected some content of a certain type. This event can help you identify popular content and categories of content on your website or app.

parameters.name: content_type
parameters.description: The type of selected content.

parameters.name: content_id
parameters.description: An identifier for the content that was selected. The value must include at least one non-numeric character.

description: Use this event when a user has shared content.

parameters.name: method
parameters.description: The method in which the content is shared.

parameters.name: content_type
parameters.description: The type of shared content.

parameters.name: item_id
parameters.description: The ID of the shared content.

description: This event indicates that a user has signed up for an account. Use this event to understand the different behaviors of logged in and logged out users.

parameters.name: method
parameters.description: The method used for sign up.

description: This event measures the sale of virtual goods in your app and helps you identify which virtual goods are the most popular.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The value of the virtual currency.

parameters.name: virtual_currency_name
parameters.description: The name of the virtual currency.

parameters.name: item_name
parameters.description: The name of the item the virtual currency is being used for.

description: This event signifies the start of the on-boarding process. Use this in a funnel with tutorial_complete to understand how many users complete the tutorial.
This event signifies the user's completion of your on-boarding process. Use this in a funnel with tutorial_begin to understand how many users complete the tutorial.
This event signifies a user has submitted their payment information in an ecommerce checkout process.

parameters.name: currency
parameters.description: Currency of the items associated with the event, in 3-letter ISO 4217 format. * Value metrics on the view_item event to not contribute to revenue * If you set value then currency is required for revenue metrics to be computed accurately.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The monetary value of the event. * Set value to the sum of (price * quantity) for all items in items. Don't include shipping or tax. * value is typically required for meaningful reporting. If you mark the event as a key event then it's recommended you set value. * currency is required if you set value.

parameters.name: coupon
parameters.description: The coupon name/code associated with the event. Event-level and item-level coupon parameters are independent.

parameters.name: payment_type
parameters.description: The chosen method of payment.

parameters.name: items
parameters.description: The items for the event.

parameters.name: item_id
parameters.description: The ID of the item. *One of item_id or item_name is required.

parameters.name: item_name
parameters.description: The name of the item. *One of item_id or item_name is required.

parameters.name: affiliation
parameters.description: A product affiliation to designate a supplying company or brick and mortar store location. Note: affiliation is only available at the item-scope.

parameters.name: coupon
parameters.description: The coupon name/code associated with the item. Event-level and item-level coupon parameters are independent.

parameters.name: discount
parameters.description: The unit monetary discount value associated with the item.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index/position of the item in a list.

parameters.name: item_brand
parameters.description: The brand of the item.

parameters.name: item_category
parameters.description: The category of the item. If used as part of a category hierarchy or taxonomy then this will be the first category.

parameters.name: item_category2
parameters.description: The second category hierarchy or additional taxonomy for the item.

parameters.name: item_category3
parameters.description: The third category hierarchy or additional taxonomy for the item.

parameters.name: item_category4
parameters.description: The fourth category hierarchy or additional taxonomy for the item.

parameters.name: item_category5
parameters.description: The fifth category hierarchy or additional taxonomy for the item.

parameters.name: item_list_id
parameters.description: The ID of the list in which the item was presented to the user. If set, event-level item_list_id is ignored. If not set, event-level item_list_id is used, if present.

parameters.name: item_list_name
parameters.description: The name of the list in which the item was presented to the user. If set, event-level item_list_name is ignored. If not set, event-level item_list_name is used, if present.

parameters.name: item_variant
parameters.description: The item variant or unique code or description for additional item details/options.

parameters.name: location_id
parameters.example: ChIJIQBpAG2ahYAR_6128GcTUEo (the Google Place ID for San Francisco)
parameters.description: The physical location associated with the item (e.g. the physical store location). It's recommended to use the Google Place ID that corresponds to the associated item. A custom location ID can also be used. Note: location_id is only available at the item-scope.

parameters.name: price
parameters.description: The monetary unit price of the item, in units of the specified currency parameter. If a discount applies to the item, set price to the discounted unit price and specify the unit price discount in the discount parameter.

parameters.name: quantity
parameters.description: Item quantity. If not set, quantity is set to 1.

description: This event signifies when one or more items is purchased by a user.

parameters.name: currency
parameters.description: Currency of the items associated with the event, in 3-letter ISO 4217 format. * Value metrics on the view_item event to not contribute to revenue * If you set value then currency is required for revenue metrics to be computed accurately.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The monetary value of the event. * Set value to the sum of (price * quantity) for all items in items. Don't include shipping or tax. * value is typically required for meaningful reporting. If you mark the event as a key event then it's recommended you set value. * currency is required if you set value.

parameters.name: customer_type
parameters.description: Is the conversion from a `new` or `returning` customer? new: New customer who hasn't purchased within a given time period (540-day window recommended and set at default, but not required). returning: A returning customer who has purchased during the given time period. Don't specify a value if there's uncertainty (for example, if the user checked out as a guest).

parameters.name: transaction_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of a transaction. The transaction_id parameter helps you avoid getting duplicate events for a purchase.

parameters.name: coupon
parameters.description: The coupon name/code associated with the event. Event-level and item-level coupon parameters are independent.

parameters.name: shipping
parameters.description: Shipping cost associated with a transaction.

parameters.name: tax
parameters.description: Tax cost associated with a transaction.

parameters.name: items
parameters.description: The items for the event.

parameters.name: item_id
parameters.description: The ID of the item. *One of item_id or item_name is required.

parameters.name: item_name
parameters.description: The name of the item. *One of item_id or item_name is required.

parameters.description: The third category hierarchy or additional taxonomy for the item.

parameters.description: The fourth category hierarchy or additional taxonomy for the item.

parameters.description: The fifth category hierarchy or additional taxonomy for the item.

parameters.description: The monetary unit price of the item, in units of the specified currency parameter. If a discount applies to the item, set price to the discounted unit price and specify the unit price discount in the discount parameter.

parameters.name: quantity
parameters.description: Item quantity. If not set, quantity is set to 1.

description: This event signifies that a user viewed their cart.

parameters.name: currency
parameters.description: Currency of the items associated with the event, in 3-letter ISO 4217 format. * Value metrics on the view_item event to not contribute to revenue * If you set value then currency is required for revenue metrics to be computed accurately.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The monetary value of the event. * Set value to the sum of (price * quantity) for all items in items. Don't include shipping or tax. * value is typically required for meaningful reporting. If you mark the event as a key event then it's recommended you set value. * currency is required if you set value.

description: This event signifies that a player has reached the end of a level in a game.

parameters.name: level_name
parameters.description: The name of the level.

parameters.name: success
parameters.description: Set to true if the level was completed successfully.

description: This event signifies that a player has started a level in a game.

parameters.name: level_name
parameters.description: The name of the level.

description: This event signifies that a player has leveled up in a game. Use it to gauge the level distribution of your user base and identify levels that are difficult to complete.

parameters.name: level
parameters.description: The level of the character.

parameters.name: character
parameters.description: The character that leveled up.

description: Send this event when the user posts a score. Use this event to understand how users are performing in your game and correlate high scores with audiences or behaviors.

parameters.name: score
parameters.description: The score to post.

parameters.name: level
parameters.description: The level for the score.

parameters.name: character
parameters.description: The character that achieved the score.

description: Log this event when the user has unlocked an achievement. This event can help you understand how users are experiencing your game.

parameters.name: achievement_id
parameters.description: The id of the achievement that was unlocked.

description: Log this event when a qualified lead is successfully converted into a customer. This typically signifies the end of the lead nurturing process, such as when a contract is signed, a paid subscription starts, or a final sale is completed.

parameters.name: currency
parameters.description: Currency of the value associated with the conversion (such as contract value), in 3-letter ISO 4217 format. Required if value is set.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The monetary value of the conversion, such as first payment amount or contract value. Required if currency is set.

parameters.name: item_category3
parameters.description: The third category hierarchy or additional taxonomy for the item.

parameters.name: item_category4
parameters.description: The fourth category hierarchy or additional taxonomy for the item.

parameters.name: item_category5
parameters.description: The fifth category hierarchy or additional taxonomy for the item.

parameters.name: item_list_id
parameters.description: The ID of the list in which the item was presented to the user. If set, event-level item_list_id is ignored. If not set, event-level item_list_id is used, if present.

parameters.name: item_list_name
parameters.description: The name of the list in which the item was presented to the user. If set, event-level item_list_name is ignored. If not set, event-level item_list_name is used, if present.

parameters.name: item_variant
parameters.description: The item variant or unique code or description for additional item details/options.

parameters.name: location_id
parameters.example: ChIJIQBpAG2ahYAR_6128GcTUEo (the Google Place ID for San Francisco)
parameters.description: The physical location associated with the item (e.g. the physical store location). It's recommended to use the Google Place ID that corresponds to the associated item. A custom location ID can also be used. Note: location_id is only available at the item-scope.

parameters.name: price
parameters.description: The monetary unit price of the item, in units of the specified currency parameter. If a discount applies to the item, set price to the discounted unit price and specify the unit price discount in the discount parameter.

parameters.name: quantity
parameters.description: Item quantity. If not set, quantity is set to 1.

description: This event measures when a lead has been generated (for example, through a form). Log this to understand the effectiveness of your marketing campaigns and how many customers re-engage with your business after remarketing to the customers.

parameters.name: currency
parameters.description: Currency of the value of the event, in 3-letter ISO 4217 format. * If you set value then currency is required for revenue metrics to be computed accurately.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The monetary value of the event. * value is typically required for meaningful reporting. If you mark the event as a key event then it's recommended you set value. * currency is required if you set value.

parameters.name: lead_source
parameters.description: The source of the lead.

parameters.name: items
parameters.description: The items associated with the event.

parameters.name: item_id
parameters.description: The ID of the item. *One of item_id or item_name is required.

parameters.name: item_name
parameters.description: The name of the item. *One of item_id or item_name is required.

parameters.name: affiliation
parameters.description: A product affiliation to designate a supplying company or brick and mortar store location. Note: affiliation is only available at the item-scope.

parameters.name: coupon
parameters.description: The coupon name/code associated with the item. Event-level and item-level coupon parameters are independent.

parameters.name: discount
parameters.description: The unit monetary discount value associated with the item.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index/position of the item in a list.

parameters.name: item_brand
parameters.description: The brand of the item.

parameters.name: item_category
parameters.description: The category of the item. If used as part of a category hierarchy or taxonomy then this will be the first category.

parameters.name: item_category2
parameters.description: The second category hierarchy or additional taxonomy for the item.

parameters.name: item_category3
parameters.description: The third category hierarchy or additional taxonomy for the item.

parameters.name: item_category4
parameters.description: The fourth category hierarchy or additional taxonomy for the item.

parameters.description: The fifth category hierarchy or additional taxonomy for the item.

parameters.description: The ID of the list in which the item was presented to the user. If set, event-level item_list_id is ignored. If not set, event-level item_list_id is used, if present.

parameters.name: item_list_name
parameters.description: The name of the list in which the item was presented to the user. If set, event-level item_list_name is ignored. If not set, event-level item_list_name is used, if present.

parameters.name: item_variant
parameters.description: The item variant or unique code or description for additional item details/options.

parameters.description: The physical location associated with the item (e.g. the physical store location). It's recommended to use the Google Place ID that corresponds to the associated item. A custom location ID can also be used. Note: location_id is only available at the item-scope.
